comments.author: @kelvincarlyle6660
comments.text: Are British people allowed to migrate there ?

comments.author: @kelvincarlyle6660
comments.text: An excellent video , pity about Napoleon. One is amazed about all the Cars , which would have been imported from so far away anyway .

comments.author: @NovaTerrain-4K
comments.text: The discovery of the remains of thousands of liberated Africans in Rupert's Valley is incredibly heavy. While the British Navy technically "liberated" them from slave ships, many ended up dying there anyway due to the horrific conditions of their journey. Should this dark history be highlighted more on a global scale as a tragic monument of the transatlantic slave trade, rather than having the island's identity dominated almost entirely by the legend of Napoleon?

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you for the kind words, it really is a special place! ❤️

comments.author: @jacklane6076
comments.text: I visited St Helena in 2004. A peaceful Island which felt kind of cultural . A great place to just sit and reflect😘

replies.author: @jacklane6076
replies.text: @SamMikael I climbed Jacob’s Ladder, Visited Napoleons tomb. I remember visiting a stately home, and I’m pretty sure I went to a museum. I’d love to visit again, but not sure my aging knees would cope with the ladder now🤭

comments.author: @UdoSchade
comments.text: Die KI-Stimme ist Schrott - eine Zumutung. Zudem habe ich hier zum ersten Mal gehört, dass eine KI-Stimme immer wieder  stottern kann. Daumen runter !

replies.author: @lemuret69
replies.text: The AI voice isn't that bad compared to others I've heard, but the channel's replies to comments also seem AI-generated, which is weirdly unnerving.

comments.author: @sool-mozika
comments.text: They didn't not die of illness, they were killed and those were mass graves!!!

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: The best part about this channel is that every video feels like a real journey instead of a simple travel guide. I always learn something new while enjoying the scenery.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: We’re so happy to hear that our storytelling approach resonates with you! 😊🌍

comments.author: @walterlv01
comments.text: Might be a great place to disappear to, but if anyone finds out you are there, you're basically effed because there's no way to escape from it!!

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: I know people say the Apollo 11 site is preserved, but part of me still thinks we should send one modern camera back there just to shut down every argument for good.

comments.author: @Jetmab04
comments.text: What a beautiful place! Perfect to just exist for a little while. I adore small islands on our planet ❤

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: We’re so glad you enjoyed it! ❤️ Saint Helena feels like a world quietly separated from everything else. Could you imagine living on an island this isolated for several months—and what part of island life do you think you would enjoy most?

comments.author: @tamenui-nuseb8758
comments.text: This is the best and honest documentary about St Helena I've seen. I didn't know about the graves of our ancestors, they made me emotional. Thank you for this.

replies.author: @tamenui-nuseb8758
replies.text: @trueglobe66 yes, their is lot of direct coobections with Island, through english colonialism. is there a way for us to share this more privately?

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you for sharing something so deeply personal. Knowing that the graves of your ancestors were part of this history makes your words especially meaningful to us. We’re honored that the documentary helped bring their story closer to you. Does your family have a direct connection to Saint Helena, or have any stories about the ancestors buried there been passed down through generations?

comments.author: @WanyuAdele
comments.text:
Nice Documentary🎉
How can i get in touch with you ?

comments.author: @Ralph-l4r
comments.text: English East India Company? It was the British East India Company.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Was there any other historical detail in the documentary that you think deserves further clarification or deeper exploration?

comments.author: @Ralph-l4r
comments.text: It would seem to be a good place for a fresh start.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: It really does feel like the kind of place where life could slow down and begin again. What would attract you most to a fresh start there—the isolation, the close-knit community, or the peaceful natural surroundings?

comments.author: @trueglobe66
